title: hieracium rauzense subsp. gletschense
intro: Hieracium rauzense subsp. gletschense, also known as Gletscher-Haarstrang, is a rare subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. It is native to the high-altitude regions of Europe, where it grows in rocky areas and alpine meadows. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in treating various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Hieracium rauzense subsp. gletschense are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
